items.title: Critical Remote Code Execution in Amasty Extension
items.summary: Sansec reported a critical vulnerability in one of 25 Amasty Magento extensions that could lead to remote code execution. Amasty has issued a massive security release to address this and other issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento REST API (PolyShell)
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 with a fix, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability
items.summary: A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) flaw in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source allows high-privileged attackers to bypass security features and access unauthorized internal or external resources. This vulnerability has a critical impact.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source to address multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.
